metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Media Arts 11 develops students as purposeful media artists who use digital and technological tools to create works that communicate ideas, emotions, and cultural perspectives. Students explore photography, video, digital design, animation, and multimedia production — developing both technical proficiency and artistic vision. The course examines how media art shapes and reflects culture, identity, and power. Indigenous media arts and perspectives on digital representation are woven throughout. Students develop a portfolio of original work demonstrating creative growth.
metadata.general_description: A senior media arts course developing technical skill, artistic vision, and critical understanding across photography, video, digital design, and multimedia — building a creative portfolio and cultural literacy.

big_ideas.statement: Exploring and creating through the arts develops our capacity to communicate and express our understanding of the world.
big_ideas.elaboration: Media art provides students with powerful tools for communication — blending technical skill with artistic vision to create works that provoke, inspire, and connect.

big_ideas.statement: Skilled artists intentionally select and combine elements of their art form to express their vision.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students learn to make deliberate, informed choices about media, tools, composition, and narrative strategy to realize their artistic intent.

big_ideas.statement: Artists work within and across communities to create, share, and respond to art.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students explore how media art operates within cultural and economic contexts, how artists collaborate and respond to audience, and how Indigenous artists use digital media for cultural sovereignty.

big_ideas.statement: Engaging with the arts expands our understanding of identity and culture.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students develop critical media literacy alongside their creative practice — understanding how media art constructs representations of identity, culture, and social reality.

competencies.raw: Create purposeful media art works using a range of digital and technological tools; apply elements and principles of design and media art composition; demonstrate developing technical proficiency in chosen media art forms; build and curate a portfolio of original media art work.
i_can_statements: I can create purposeful, technically proficient media artworks using digital tools and technologies.
I can apply elements of design — line, shape, colour, texture, space — and composition principles to my media art.
I can develop a coherent creative process from concept to finished work, including planning, production, and reflection.
I can curate a portfolio of original media art that demonstrates growth, range, and artistic vision.
I can adapt and troubleshoot technical challenges in the production process.

competencies.raw: Analyze and evaluate media artworks using art and media literacy frameworks; connect works to their cultural, historical, and social contexts; reflect on personal creative growth and artistic intentions.
i_can_statements: I can analyze media artworks — my own and others' — using design, composition, and media literacy frameworks.
I can connect media artworks to their cultural, historical, and social contexts — including Indigenous media sovereignty.
I can give and receive constructive feedback on media art with specificity and respect.
I can reflect critically on my creative process, artistic choices, and growth as a media artist.

areas.name: Photography
areas.raw: Digital photography: technique, composition, and artistic expression.
topics: Camera fundamentals: aperture, shutter speed, ISO, and exposure triangle
Composition principles: rule of thirds, leading lines, framing, depth of field, and visual flow
Lighting: natural light, artificial light, and the use of light to shape mood and meaning
Portrait and documentary photography: ethical considerations and technical approach
Photo editing: Adobe Lightroom, Photoshop, or GIMP for colour correction and artistic editing
Photo essay: creating a cohesive series of images that tells a visual story

areas.name: Video and Film Production
areas.raw: Video production, cinematography, and digital filmmaking.
topics: Cinematography: camera angles, movements, and their emotional/narrative effects
Pre-production: concept development, scripting, storyboarding, and production planning
Production: directing, filming, and managing a video shoot
Post-production: non-linear editing, colour grading, sound design, and visual effects
Short film and music video production: applying narrative and visual skills to short-form video
Screen language: studying how professional films use visual storytelling

areas.name: Digital Design and Illustration
areas.raw: Graphic design, digital illustration, and visual communication.
topics: Graphic design principles: hierarchy, contrast, alignment, proximity, and repetition
Typography: type selection, layout, and typographic expression
Digital illustration: vector and raster illustration using Illustrator, Procreate, or similar tools
Branding and identity design: logos, colour systems, and visual language
Layout and publication design: poster, zine, and editorial design
Accessible and inclusive design: designing for diverse audiences and abilities

areas.name: Animation and Motion Graphics
areas.raw: 2D animation, motion graphics, and interactive media.
topics: Principles of animation: squash and stretch, anticipation, staging, and follow-through
Frame-by-frame animation: traditional and digital 2D animation techniques
Motion graphics: animating text, shapes, and graphic elements for digital media
Stop motion: designing and producing stop motion animation sequences
Character design: developing original animated characters with personality and visual consistency
Animation software: introduction to tools such as Adobe Animate or Procreate Animation

areas.name: Media Art, Culture, and Identity
areas.raw: Critical media art analysis, Indigenous media arts, and social context.
topics: Media art history: key movements, artists, and technological shifts from photography to digital art
Indigenous media arts: First Peoples artists using digital media for cultural expression and sovereignty
Representation and bias: how media art constructs identity, race, gender, and culture
Media art and social change: using art to comment on and challenge social issues
Copyright and intellectual property in digital art: Creative Commons and artist rights
Professional media art pathways: careers in film, design, game art, and visual media
